content.problemStatement:
Arya and Aryan live in a country called Nadaca. Nadaca consists of NNN cities numbered 111 through NNN, which are connected by some bidirectional roads. Each road has a positive (not necessarily integer) length. Arya lives in city 111 and Aryan lives in city NNN.

Arya claims that for each city vvv, the shortest path from city 111 to city vvv has length ava_vav​. Aryan does not disagree, but claims that for each city vvv, the shortest path from city NNN to city vvv has length bvb_vbv​. You are the wisest person they know, so they asked you to tell them if it is possible for their claims to be true, i.e. if a road network which fully satisfies their claims exists. Help them!
constraints: 0≤ai,bi≤1090 \le a_i, b_i \le 10^90≤ai​,bi​≤109 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers b1,b2,…,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1​,b2​,…,bN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Yes"` if Arya's and Aryan's claims can be true or `"No"` otherwise.
